bird.name: Buff-rumped thornbill
bird.summary: The buff-rumped thornbill (Acanthiza reguloides) is a small passerine bird species belonging to the genus Acanthiza, most of which are ende…

locations.name: Chinchilla, Queensland
locations.summary: Chinchilla is a rural town and locality in the Western Downs Region, Queensland, Australia.

locations.name: Cobar
locations.summary: Cobar is a town in Outback New South Wales, Australia, whose economy is based mainly upon base metals and gold mining.

relations.subject: Buff-rumped thornbill
relations.evidence: Primarily distributed in eastern Australia, the buff-rumped thornbill's range spans from Chinchilla, Queensland, west to Cobar, New South Wales, across Victoria and southeastern South Australia. This is an area of approximately 2 000 000 km2. This adaptable species thrives in dry sclerophyll forests and open eucalypt woodlands that have an open or sparse understorey. [[File:Distribution of the Buff Rumped Thornbill in Australia.jpg|thumb|Distribution of Acanthiza reguloides within Australia
